fields.name: Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the entry, as assigned from the specified number series when the entry was created.

fields.name: Cust. Ledger Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the entry number of the customer ledger entry that the detailed customer ledger entry line was created for.

fields.name: Entry Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the entry type of the detailed customer ledger entry.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting date of the detailed customer ledger entry.

fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the document type of the detailed customer ledger entry.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the document number of the transaction that created the entry.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the detailed customer ledger entry.

fields.name: Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the entry in LCY.

fields.name: Customer No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer account number to which the entry is posted.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the currency if the amount is in a foreign currency.

fields.name: User ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the user who posted the entry, to be used, for example, in the change log.

fields.name: Source Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the source code that specifies where the entry was created.

fields.name: Transaction No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the transaction number that is assigned to all the entries in the same transaction. The program assigns this number, which links all entries that are created by the same posting.

fields.name: Journal Batch Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the journal that the entry was posted from. The program fills in the name from the Journal Batch Name on the journal line.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reason code, a supplementary source code that enables you to trace the entry.

fields.name: Debit Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent debits.

fields.name: Credit Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent credits.

fields.name: Debit Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent debits, expressed in LCY.

fields.name: Credit Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent credits, expressed in LCY.

fields.name: Initial Entry Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date on which the initial entry is due for payment.

fields.name: Initial Entry Global Dim. 1
fields.tooltip: Specifies the Global Dimension 1 code of the initial customer ledger entry.

fields.name: Initial Entry Global Dim. 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies the Global Dimension 2 code of the initial customer ledger entry.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the posting group of the entry.
The posting group is copied from the Gen. Bus. Posting Group field on the vendor card.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the general product account in the general ledger to which customer transactions involving the item are posted.

Additional Information

When you post transactions for this item, this code is used with a posting group code in the Inventory Posting Setup window. The accounts in which the postings for sales, discounts, and so on are made, the VAT rate, and the VAT calculation type are determined in the Inventory Posting Setup window.

fields.name: Use Tax
fields.tooltip:
Indicates that the tax amount for the entry consists of use tax.
The value is copied from the Use Tax field on the sales order line.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the VAT business posting group of the customer.

fields.name: VAT Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the VAT product posting group of the customer.

fields.name: Initial Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the document type that the initial customer ledger entry was created with.

fields.name: Applied Cust. Ledger Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer ledger entry number that the entry is applied to.

fields.name: Unapplied
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the entry has been unapplied (undone) from the Unapply Customer Entries window by the entry no. shown in the Unapplied by Entry No. field.

fields.name: Unapplied by Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the correcting entry, if the original entry has been unapplied (undone) from the Unapply Customer Entries window.

fields.name: Remaining Pmt. Disc. Possible
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the remaining payment discount that is available if the entry is totally applied within the payment period.
When you post, the field is calculated from the following fields:

If the entry was posted from a journal line, the possible discount is calculated according to the Payment Discount % field on the journal line.


If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the possible discount is calculated according to the Payment Discount % field on the sales header.


You can also change the remaining payment discount amount manually but only when it has not been applied.

fields.name: Max. Payment Tolerance
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the maximum tolerated amount that the amount in the entry can differ from the amount on the invoice or credit memo.
The Payment Tolerance % field and the Max. Payment Tolerance Amount field are used in the General Ledger Setup table to calculate the payment tolerance.

fields.name: Tax Jurisdiction Code
fields.tooltip: Displays the tax jurisdiction that determined the tax accounts and the tax details that were used to calculate the tax amount.

fields.name: Application No.
fields.tooltip: There is no Help for the fields in this table. This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's market type to link business transactions to.
